summary: Full backend + frontend testing of RODDOS Contable IA hybrid design + Alegra real connection. Core features work correctly. 2 notable bugs found.

critical.issue: Alegra real API returns 403 Forbidden for this account. Backend returns resp.json() = {'message': 'Forbidden'} as 200 OK instead of raising error. Frontend flattenAccounts() tries to iterate over dict → TypeError: accs is not iterable on every page load.

ui_bugs.issue: Status badges (Estado column) have CSS designed for dark mode: text-blue-300 on bg-blue-900/40. In hybrid white-content layout the badge text is nearly invisible (low contrast on white). Only 'closed' status (dark bg) is visible.

ui_bugs.component: FacturacionVenta / FacturacionCompra — Estado column in Compra
ui_bugs.issue: In Facturación Compra the Estado column badges also show empty-looking pills (same dark-mode CSS issue).

ui_bugs.component: Estado Resultados — Egresos table Provider column
ui_bugs.issue: In Estado Resultados, the Egresos table shows '—' in the Provider column instead of actual provider names (e.g., AUTOTECNICA COLOMBIANA S.A.S).

issues: Status badge CSS uses dark-mode color scheme (text-blue-300, bg-blue-900/40) that is near-invisible on the new white content background (#F8FAFC)

action_items: FIX HIGH: Handle 403 in alegra_service.py _call() method (add: if resp.status_code == 403: return [] or raise appropriate error). Currently returns dict {message: Forbidden} → TypeError in flattenAccounts.

FIX MEDIUM: Update status badge CSS in index.css (.status-open, .status-paid, .status-overdue, .status-draft, .status-voided) to use light-mode appropriate colors that are visible on white background.
FIX LOW: In Estado Resultados Egresos table, use provider.name from bill data to populate Provider column.
critical_code_review_comments: alegra_service.py line 60: returns resp.json() for ANY non-401/429/5xx status. Must handle 403 → return [] or raise 403.
index.css status-* classes use dark-mode Tailwind classes incompatible with light (#F8FAFC) content background.
FacturacionVenta.js line 42: 'toast' not imported but used in catch block. Would throw ReferenceError if API fails (though finally still runs).

success_rate.backend: 90% (invoices, bills, test-connection, contacts, payments all work. Only accounts API returning 403 from Alegra)
success_rate.frontend: 80% (dashboard, facturación venta/compra with data, estado resultados, navigation, login all work. Status badges low contrast issue)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Key findings: 1) Alegra IS connected (RODDOS SAS), badge shows 'Alegra conectado' green. 2) Real data loading: Dashboard shows FE452/FE451 invoices + AUTOTECNICA bills. Facturación Venta loads 21 real invoices (needs ~12s). Facturación Compra loads real bills with Excel button. Estado Resultados has PDF+Excel buttons with real KPIs. 3) Bug: /api/alegra/accounts returns 403 from Alegra → backend passes dict to frontend → TypeError accs not iterable (console error on every page). 4) Bug: Status badge CSS (dark-mode colors) shows near-invisible text on white content background. 5) FacturacionVenta.js uses 'toast' without importing it.
rca_of_issue: 1) Accounts 403: Alegra API requires specific plan/permissions for chart of accounts endpoint. Backend only catches 401/429/5xx but not 403, so returns error dict as 200. Fix: add 'if resp.status_code == 403: return []' in alegra_service.py _call(). 2) Status badges: When redesign changed content background from dark to white (#F8FAFC), the status CSS classes were not updated to use light-mode compatible colors. Fix: change .status-open to use bg-blue-100 text-blue-700, etc.
